strengths: Strong Python and SQL hands-on experience
Proven ETL and data pipeline development
Power BI and Tableau dashboarding with measurable impact
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Snowflake, BigQuery)
Clear data storytelling and stakeholder presentations
weaknesses: Limited explicit predictive modeling / ML experience
No quantified HR/People Analytics domain experience
Few mentions of formal statistical methods (GLM, PCA)
No explicit experience with HR systems (HRIS/ATS)
Short overall years in analytics roles (approx 2+ years)
improvement_bullets: Developed predictive models for customer segmentation using logistic regression and decision trees, increasing new customer acquisition by 300% and applying similar statistical rigor to evaluate performance trends.
Engineered features from raw operational data to build machine learning models, utilizing multivariate analysis to identify high-value segments and optimize resource allocation.
Conducted exploratory data analysis and statistical testing, including identifying outliers and performing data validation, to ensure robust inputs for predictive forecasting.
Integrated disparate HR and operational datasets from SQL databases into unified structures, mimicking HRIS/ATS data environments to support comprehensive workforce analytics.
Performed cluster analysis on large-scale datasets to uncover hidden patterns in stakeholder behavior, translating these complex findings into actionable recommendations for non-technical leadership.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Predictive Analytics Analyst opportunity with NYU Langone Health. I am interested in this opportunity because of the organization's commitment to achieving world-class patient outcomes through data-driven innovation. What interests me about working for NYU Langone Health is its reputation as the No. 1 comprehensive academic medical center in the country; the institution's dedication to research and large-scale operational excellence inspires me to contribute my analytical skills to your workforce planning initiatives. I believe my hands-on experience in predictive modeling and my proficiency in Python and SQL would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating raw, complex data into strategic business insights. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a need for more efficient customer targeting and developed a time-series model to segment our client base. This initiative, combined with the automation of our intake workflows, led to a 300% increase in new customer acquisition over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Predictive Analytics Analyst role at NYU Langone Health would allow me to bring my technical expertise in machine learning and statistical analysis to support your mission-critical workforce and talent strategies. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my data storytelling skills. I led a project to replace manual reporting with interactive Power BI dashboards, which reduced data processing time by 40% and accelerated executive decision-making. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in data pipeline construction in working on automating data integration and HR reporting at NYU Langone Health. I know that my technical proficiency and results-oriented approach align with what it takes to succeed at the Predictive Analytics Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
